title: Cloud storage · Data Lake
subtitle: S3 ≈ Azure Blob ≈ GCS · lake zones · IAM · lifecycle · partition · cost

levels.title: Object storage + lake zones
levels.instruction: On interviews: one pattern — different names per cloud.

layers.label: bronze / raw
layers.sublabel: raw CSV · JSON · logs as landed

bullets: Object storage ≠ database: files + prefixes, no JOIN in S3
Parquet in lake — compression + column pushdown in Spark/BQ/Synapse
levels.recallMapRef: 📍 Bridge: DevOps K8s · K0 (Deployment → Pod — where ETL/service runs).

levels.title: MATCH · cloud ↔ term
levels.instruction: One concept — three vendors. Know the equivalents.

levels.title: Lake path · partition
levels.instruction: Raw daily orders in bronze, partitioned by date.

mission.title: Bucket layout
mission.brief: Company stores parquet by day. Analysts read only the needed prefix — lower cost.
mission.ask: Fill lake zone and partition keys in the path.

columnGlossary.prefix: Folder prefix in bucket (not a new bucket per day)
columnGlossary.zone: bronze / silver / gold — data maturity stage

sampleTable.caption: Hive-style: year= / month= / day= in path — partition, not a column in every file.

levels.controlTip: Mission and zone table above. Path below.

levels.title: What's wrong with this policy?
levels.instruction: Classic junior DE trap on AWS.

mission.title: Security review
mission.brief: ETL role should read bronze and write silver. No public access.

levels.prompt: Main risk in this bucket policy?
options: Principal * + s3:* on whole bucket = public full access; need IAM role for ETL and minimal Action/Resource
s3:* must be replaced with s3:GetObject only for Glacier
levels.explainOk: Least privilege: service role, specific prefixes (bronze/* read, silver/* write), no Principal *.
levels.explanation: On interview: «enable public access block + role-based access».

levels.title: Lifecycle · cost
levels.instruction: Data older than 90 days is rarely read but must stay for audit.

mission.title: FinOps
mission.brief: 1 TB logs in Standard. Read once per quarter. What do you do?

levels.question: Smartest lifecycle for rarely read raw logs?
options: Rule: after 90 days → Glacier / Archive tier; hot only last month
Delete everything older than 7 days without compliance sign-off
Copy to RDS Postgres for cheaper storage
levels.explanation: Lifecycle / tiering lowers cost; compliance → no delete without policy.

levels.title: S3 or RDS?
levels.scene: PM: «We'll store 500M events/year and sometimes JOIN with users». Where do raw events go?

choices.label: RDS Postgres only — JOIN there
choices.feedback: Expensive and poor scale for hundreds of millions of raw log rows.

choices.label: Raw → object storage (parquet, partition by day); users in RDS/DWH; JOIN in warehouse (BQ/Snowflake/Spark), not in S3 directly
choices.feedback: Typical lake + warehouse pattern on interviews.

levels.title: Order: ingest → lake
levels.instruction: Arrange batch pipeline steps in object storage (logic only, no Airflow yet).
